todaysEvents.summary: Drone struck fuel tank at Dubai International Airport early Mar 16, suspending all Emirates flights; UAE simultaneously hit by 6 ballistic missiles and 21 drones; fire reported at Fujairah oil facility — widening Iran proxy campaign to civilian air infrastructure. (Mar 16)

Trump's proposed international warship coalition to secure Hormuz drew a muted response: Japan PM Takaichi confirmed Japan has no plans to send vessels; no country publicly committed; Iran FM reiterated Hormuz 'closed only to our enemies,' not to neutral shipping. (Mar 16)
todaysEvents.delta: Coalition collapses; Iran maintains selective access

todaysEvents.summary: Single non-Iranian Aframax tanker (Karachi, carrying Abu Dhabi crude) transited Hormuz with AIS active on Mar 16 — first confirmed negotiated passage — while Iran conducted a significant cyberattack on US-based Stryker medical equipment, taking tens of thousands of devices offline. (Mar 16)
todaysEvents.delta: One tanker transits; Iran cyberattack on US

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran's selective-access framework — allowing neutral countries bilateral transit negotiations — provides a partial functional corridor. Brent oscillates $100-110/bbl as insured escorted traffic remains thin but some non-hostile nations secure passage. Trump coalition fails to materialise beyond US assets, but UAE/Saudi military containment holds. Qatar partial LNG restart continues; IEA reserve release sustains near-term crude supply. ASEAN LNG buyers source through Australia and US spot. P&I cover does not return broadly until a formal ceasefire framework is signed.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Bilateral transit deals cover only select non-hostile nations; commercial charterers cannot secure insurance for most flag states. Dubai Airport attack triggers sovereign risk re-rating for Gulf hub operators. Brent trades $105-125/bbl band as Fujairah disruption adds loading delays. Basra and Gulf loadings intermittent; Qatar restart stays partial. ASEAN oil-importer FX (THB, PHP, IDR) under renewed pressure as current-account deterioration accelerates. Bank Indonesia, BSP, and BOT remain on intervention watch rather than easing.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Dubai Airport and Fujairah attacks signal Iran targeting Gulf civilian and energy infrastructure broadly. Alliance escalation draws in additional actors; GCC cooperation with US intensifies but fails to deter. Hormuz bilateral transit deals collapse; full commercial blockade reinstated. Oil hits $150-180/bbl; LNG spot markets seize for Asian buyers. ASEAN recession risk surges; Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and potential credit-rating action. P&I cover unavailable across the region for multiple quarters.
